observation.summary: Closed-loop maturity work introduced meaning-bound DSL, runner, generated evidence, workflow, tests, and loop registry files, but no gate forced future changes to keep those semantic peers aligned.

hypothesis.summary: A compact semantic binding manifest can make trigger files name the required evidence peers that must change with them, so an agent cannot silently update only one side of a claim.

execution.summary: Read changed files from staged git diff or explicit CI inputs, evaluate each semantic binding, and emit evidence JSON with missing peer paths.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects a closed-loop maturity trigger change unless its test, generated evidence, local QA workflow, and loop-engineering entry are changed together.

retrospective.summary: The first binding closes the gap between harness maturity claims and the generated/evidence surfaces that prove them; future claims can be added without changing API/runtime contracts.

evidence.proves: closed-loop maturity trigger changes fail when required semantic peers are absent
public CI preserves positive and negative semantic binding evidence
